rationale: Moderate seat dispersion (0.67) with all three readers converging on 'high' or 'crowded' pricing — the committee agrees the scarcity thesis is real but disagrees on degree of mispricing. Bull argues duration underpricing; Bear and Skeptic argue the stocks have already captured it. Weighing seats equally, the preponderance of evidence supports 'high' (not 'crowded') because the crowded read requires a cleaner mean-reversion signal than the filings currently provide — the SEC documents cited (HNRG safety order, director board seats) are peripheral and do not constitute macro bottleneck evidence.
variant_view: What consensus misses is not existence of the shortage but its institutional stickiness: union apprenticeship pipelines run 4-5 years, journeyman hours requirements cannot be compressed, and prefab/modular offsets address civil work but not energized termination and commissioning — the highest-value, least-substitutable labor. The re-rate catalyst would be sequential, cross-company disclosure of project-delay charges or explicit wage-escalation pass-throughs in backlog guidance from EME or PWR earnings (Q2/Q3 2026), which would validate duration pricing that the market has not yet underwritten
bear_case: The steelmanned bear: at current multiples, these equities are pricing in a 5-7 year scarcity duration with no supply response — a heroic assumption. Historically, a 30-40% real wage premium (which electricians are now commanding in high-demand markets) has reliably triggered a vocational enrollment surge within 2-3 years and union local scaling within 4. Simultaneously, hyperscalers are actively funding prefab electrical skid programs and poaching international licensed electricians via visa pathways.
